title: Enable checkpoint/restore of /dev/shm content
body:
When Podman is running a container in private IPC mode (default), it creates a bind mount for `/dev/shm` that is then attached to a tmpfs folder on the host file system. However, checkpointing a container has the side-effect of stopping that container and unmount the tmpfs used for `/dev/shm`. As a result, after checkpoint all files stored in the container's `/dev/shm` would be lost and the container might fail to restore from checkpoint.

To address this problem, this pull request creates a tar file with the content of `/dev/shm` that is included in the container checkpoint and used to restore the container.

Fixes: #12277

comments.body: Changes LGTM, but one of the tests isn't happy.  I don't think it's due to these changes.  @edsantiago if you're around, can you please eyeball it?

The failure is here https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fbb7d3d20e2045cd323c8f551715c7885b4143b0/test/e2e/pod_create_test.go#L133-L134

It's not one I remember seeing before. I'm guessing that there's a conflict between the two tests in this file that use 8082.

Changes are now looking good. 

I think I have also seen that CI error before. I just re-triggered the failing job many times and one point it passes.

But I have also seen errors with hard-coded ports in tests before in checkpoint/restore. This is not for @rst0git to fix (you can if you want), but there exists a function to get a random port (`utils.GetRandomPort()`) which is also used in checkpoint/restore tests (maybe as an example).
